skill.name: system-design
skill.description: Guides system architecture work through EventStorming, Mermaid diagrams, and a five-phase design catalog workflow.
skill.summary: Create structured system design catalogs from requirements using EventStorming and Mermaid diagrams.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, C2 keywords, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, Mermaid diagram syntax, color codes, and ordinary architecture terms. No scripts, network calls, credential access, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempts were found in the single skill file. The only confirmed risk is low-impact filesystem output to a relative documentation directory.

low_findings.title: Relative Filesystem Documentation Output
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create design artifacts under docs/design-catalog/. This is expected for a documentation skill and uses relative project paths, but it can still modify the user workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names relative output paths for generated Markdown and Mermaid artifacts. The behavior is bounded to documentation output and does not include destructive operations.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution, C2, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography alerts are caused by Markdown fences, inline paths, Mermaid class names, hex color values, and architecture vocabulary. No executable Ruby, shell, cryptographic code, or command-and-control behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are rendered Markdown examples and Mermaid diagrams. They contain no runtime code path and no instruction to execute external commands.

content.value_statement: Early architecture work often drifts into assumptions or scattered notes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a disciplined EventStorming process that produces a navigable design catalog.
actual_capabilities: Guides requirements discovery with actor, constraint, goal, and integration questions.
Uses EventStorming to model domain events, commands, actors, external systems, aggregates, and hotspots.
Produces Mermaid-based big picture, process, ERD, state, and sequence diagrams.
Organizes artifacts under a standard docs/design-catalog/ structure.
Creates a README navigation hub with embedded Mermaid diagrams.
Keeps design artifacts conceptual and defers implementation details.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include executable scripts.
It depends on user answers and should not infer unknown requirements.
It creates design documentation, not production code or deployment plans.
The repository contains only SKILL.md, despite references to template files.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Turn an early product idea into requirements, domain events, entities, flows, and open design questions.

use_cases.title: Align a Delivery Team
use_cases.description: Create shared Mermaid diagrams and a catalog structure before implementation planning starts.

use_cases.title: Document a Complex Domain
use_cases.description: Use EventStorming to expose business events, process boundaries, lifecycle states, and integration hotspots.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Design Catalog
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: a user has an idea but no architecture document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the system-design skill to help me design a new system. Start by asking one requirements question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Model Business Events
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: requirements are known, but domain flow is unclear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use EventStorming to build the big picture for this system. Identify events, commands, actors, external systems, and hotspots.

prompt_templates.title: Detail Critical Processes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: the high-level design exists and key workflows need detail.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create process-level EventStorming diagrams for the two most critical workflows. Keep each diagram in Mermaid format.

prompt_templates.title: Assemble the Full Catalog
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: the user wants complete design artifacts for implementation planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce the full docs/design-catalog structure with requirements, big picture, process diagrams, data diagrams, flow diagrams, and a README with embedded diagrams.

output_examples.input: Design a subscription billing system for a small SaaS product.
output: A requirements summary with actors, constraints, success criteria, and integrations.
A Mermaid big picture EventStorming diagram covering subscription, payment, renewal, and cancellation events.
A catalog README that links and embeds the generated design artifacts.

output_examples.input: Help our team understand the order fulfillment process before coding.
output: A focused process diagram for order placement, payment, fulfillment, shipment, and exception handling.
Conceptual entity relationships for order, customer, payment, inventory item, and shipment.
Hotspots for unclear policies such as refunds, inventory sync, and retry behavior.

output_examples.input: Create design artifacts for a workflow with several state transitions.
output: State diagrams for entities with meaningful lifecycle changes.
Sequence diagrams for critical success and error paths.
A short next-step section for implementation planning.

best_practices: Answer discovery questions before asking for diagrams or catalog output.
Review each phase before moving to the next phase.
Mark unknown decisions as hotspots instead of accepting assumptions.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to generate implementation code, database migrations, or deployment files.
Skipping EventStorming and asking for generic architecture prose.
Creating diagrams for every possible flow instead of the critical workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code?
faq.answer: No. It creates conceptual design documentation and Mermaid diagrams for architecture planning.

faq.question: Can it choose the technology stack for me?
faq.answer: It should ask about constraints first. Unknown technology choices should be marked as hotspots.

faq.question: Where are generated artifacts placed?
faq.answer: The skill uses a relative docs/design-catalog/ directory in the current project workspace.

faq.question: Does it require local scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill file states that it provides templates and guidance without local script execution.

faq.question: What diagram format does it use?
faq.answer: It requires Mermaid diagrams for big picture, process, data, state, and sequence views.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found low risk only, limited to expected relative documentation file output.
